idea.hook: This Dwarf Planet Spins So Fast...
facts: Haumea, a dwarf planet in the Kuiper Belt, is shaped like a squashed football.
It completes a full rotation in just 3.9 hours – one of the fastest spins in our Solar System for its size.
This incredible speed distorts its rocky body, flattening it into an ellipsoid about twice as long as it is wide.
idea.payoff: Its rapid spin literally stretches it into a unique, non-spherical cosmic rugby ball!
idea.title: Haumea: The Cosmic Football! 🤯🪐

scenes.text: This Dwarf Planet Spins So Fast...
scenes.visual: Dark cosmic background with distant stars. A faint, blurry streak or rapidly spinning abstract light appears, hinting at extreme speed.

scenes.text: Meet Haumea: A dwarf planet in the Kuiper Belt.
scenes.visual: A detailed 3D model of Haumea appears, initially looking somewhat spherical but slightly squashed, slowly rotating against a background of faint, icy Kuiper Belt objects.
Haumea spins rapidly on its axis. A circular arrow graphic animates around it, indicating fast rotation. The text '3.9 hours' flashes prominently.

scenes.text: This speed flattens it: Twice as long as it is wide!
scenes.visual: Haumea's model visibly stretches and flattens into a distinct ellipsoid shape while continuing its rapid spin. On-screen text or graphic indicators visually demonstrate the '2x long as 1x wide' ratio.

scenes.text: A unique, non-spherical cosmic rugby ball!
scenes.visual: The final, highly elongated, rugby-ball-shaped Haumea spins dramatically, showcasing its unique form. Subtle light glints off its icy surface, highlighting its contours.
